title: swipe
description: Swipe (or scroll) a surface in a direction or between two points. The direction is the virtual finger's motion: swiping up moves content up, revealing content further down the page. Works on app and browser surfaces.

direction.title: Swipe (simple)
direction.description: Direction the virtual finger moves. `up` reveals content below; `left` reveals content to the right (for example, the next carousel card).

x.description: Horizontal position as a fraction of the surface's width (0 = left edge, 1 = right edge).

y.description: Vertical position as a fraction of the surface's height (0 = top edge, 1 = bottom edge).

duration.description: Duration of the swipe movement in milliseconds.

surface.description: The browser window/tab or app window this step acts on. Omit to act on the active tab. The targeted surface stays focused afterward. App surfaces use the object form ({ "app": … }).

distance.description: How far to swipe, as a fraction of the surface's height (for up/down) or width (for left/right).

from.description: Where the virtual finger presses down, as fractions of the surface's width and height.

to.description: Where the virtual finger releases, as fractions of the surface's width and height.
